017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.ReflectionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ClassUtils.*;
022import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.CollectionUtils.*;
023import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
024import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ThrowableUtils.*;
025
026import java.io.*;
027import java.lang.reflect.*;
028import java.math.*;
029import java.net.*;
030import java.text.*;
031import java.time.*;
032import java.time.Duration;
033import java.time.temporal.*;
034import java.util.*;
035import java.util.function.*;
036import java.util.stream.*;
037
038import javax.xml.datatype.*;
039
040import org.apache.juneau.commons.bean.*;
041import org.apache.juneau.commons.collections.*;
042import org.apache.juneau.commons.function.*;
043import org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.*;
044import org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.*;
045import org.apache.juneau.commons.svl.*;
046import org.apache.juneau.marshall.*;
047import org.apache.juneau.marshall.DateFormat;
048import org.apache.juneau.marshall.httppart.*;
049import org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.*;
050import org.apache.juneau.marshall.soap.*;
051import org.apache.juneau.marshall.swap.*;
052import org.apache.juneau.marshall.utils.*;
053
054/**
055 * Serializer session that lives for the duration of a single use of {@link Serializer}.
056 *
057 * <p>
058 * Used by serializers for the following purposes:
059 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
060 *    <li>
061 *       Keeping track of how deep it is in a model for indentation purposes.
062 *    <li>
063 *       Ensuring infinite loops don't occur by setting a limit on how deep to traverse a model.
064 *    <li>
065 *       Ensuring infinite loops don't occur from loops in the model (when detectRecursions is enabled.
066 *    <li>
067 *       Allowing serializer properties to be overridden on method calls.
068 * </ul>
069 *
070 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
071 *    <li class='warn'>This class is not thread safe and is typically discarded after one use.
072 * </ul>
073 *
074 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
075 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/SerializersAndParsers">Serializers and Parsers</a>
076
077 * </ul>
078 */
079@SuppressWarnings({
080   "java:S115",  // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention
081   "rawtypes",   // Raw types necessary for generic type handling throughout serializer session
082   "unchecked", // Type erasure requires unchecked casts throughout serializer session
083   "resource"   // Internal helpers return Closeables wired into pipe lifecycle; Eclipse JDT @Owning warning is by design.
084})
085public class SerializerSession extends MarshallingTraverseSession {
086
087   // Property name constants
088   private static final String PROP_javaMethod = "javaMethod";
089   private static final String PROP_resolver = "resolver";
090   private static final String PROP_schema = "schema";
091   private static final String PROP_uriContext = "uriContext";
092   private static final String PROP_uriResolver = "uriResolver";
093   private static final String PROP_keepNullProperties = "keepNullProperties";
094   private static final String PROP_nonDefault = "nonDefault";
095   private static final String PROP_trimStrings = "trimStrings";
096   private static final String PROP_addBeanTypes = "addBeanTypes";
097   private static final String PROP_addRootType = "addRootType";
098   private static final String PROP_sortCollections = "sortCollections";
099   private static final String PROP_sortMaps = "sortMaps";
100   private static final String PROP_trimEmptyCollections = "trimEmptyCollections";
101   private static final String PROP_trimEmptyMaps = "trimEmptyMaps";
102   private static final String PROP_SerializerSession_javaMethod = "SerializerSession.javaMethod";
103   private static final String PROP_SerializerSession_resolver = "SerializerSession.resolver";
104   private static final String PROP_SerializerSession_schema = "SerializerSession.schema";
105   private static final String PROP_SerializerSession_uriContext = "SerializerSession.uriContext";
106   private static final String PROP_SerializerSession_keepNullProperties = "SerializerSession.keepNullProperties";
107   private static final String PROP_SerializerSession_nonDefault = "SerializerSession.nonDefault";
108   private static final String PROP_SerializerSession_trimStrings = "SerializerSession.trimStrings";
109   private static final String PROP_SerializerSession_addBeanTypes = "SerializerSession.addBeanTypes";
110   private static final String PROP_SerializerSession_addRootType = "SerializerSession.addRootType";
111   private static final String PROP_SerializerSession_sortCollections = "SerializerSession.sortCollections";
112   private static final String PROP_SerializerSession_sortMaps = "SerializerSession.sortMaps";
113   private static final String PROP_SerializerSession_trimEmptyCollections = "SerializerSession.trimEmptyCollections";
114   private static final String PROP_SerializerSession_trimEmptyMaps = "SerializerSession.trimEmptyMaps";
115
116   /**
117    * Builder class.
118    */
119   @SuppressWarnings({
120      "java:S119" // 'SELF' (CRTP self-type) is intentional and clearer than a single-letter name.
121   })
122   public abstract static class Builder<SELF extends Builder<SELF>> extends MarshallingTraverseSession.Builder<SELF> {
123
124      private static final String ARG_ctx = "ctx";
125
126      private HttpPartSchema schema;
127      private Method javaMethod;
128      private Serializer ctx;
129      private UriContext uriContext;
130      private VarResolverSession resolver;
131      private boolean keepNullProperties;
132      private boolean nonDefault;
133      private boolean trimStrings;
134      private boolean addBeanTypes;
135      private boolean addRootType;
136      private boolean sortCollections;
137      private boolean sortMaps;
138      private boolean trimEmptyCollections;
139      private boolean trimEmptyMaps;
140
141      /**
142       * Constructor
143       *
144       * @param ctx The context creating this session.
145       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
146       */
147      protected Builder(Serializer ctx) {
148         super(assertArgNotNull(ARG_ctx, ctx));
149         this.ctx = ctx;
150         mediaTypeDefault(ctx.getResponseContentType());
151         uriContext = ctx.getUriContext();
152         keepNullProperties = ctx.isKeepNullProperties();
153         nonDefault = ctx.isNonDefault();
154         trimStrings = ctx.isTrimStrings();
155         addBeanTypes = ctx.isAddBeanTypes();
156         addRootType = ctx.isAddRootType();
157         sortCollections = ctx.isSortCollections();
158         sortMaps = ctx.isSortMaps();
159         trimEmptyCollections = ctx.isTrimEmptyCollections();
160         trimEmptyMaps = ctx.isTrimEmptyMaps();
161      }
162
163      @Override
164      public SerializerSession build() {
165         return new SerializerSession(this);
166      }
167
168      /**
169       * The java method that called this serializer, usually the method in a REST servlet.
170       *
171       * @param value
172       *    The new property value.
173       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (value will not be set, existing value will be kept).
174       * @return This object.
175       */
176      public SELF javaMethod(Method value) {
177         if (nn(value))
178            javaMethod = value;
179         return self();
180      }
181
182      @Override /* Overridden from Builder */
183      public SELF property(String key, Object value) {
184         if (key == null) {
185            super.property(key, value);
186            return self();
187         }
188         switch (key) {
189            case PROP_javaMethod, PROP_SerializerSession_javaMethod:
190               return javaMethod(cvt(value, Method.class));
191            case PROP_resolver, PROP_SerializerSession_resolver:
192               return resolver(cvt(value, VarResolverSession.class));
193            case PROP_schema, PROP_SerializerSession_schema:
194               return schema(cvt(value, HttpPartSchema.class));
195            case PROP_uriContext, PROP_SerializerSession_uriContext:
196               return uriContext(cvt(value, UriContext.class));
197            case PROP_keepNullProperties, PROP_SerializerSession_keepNullProperties:
198               return keepNullProperties(cvt(value, Boolean.class));
199            case PROP_nonDefault, PROP_SerializerSession_nonDefault:
200               return nonDefault(cvt(value, Boolean.class));
201            case PROP_trimStrings, PROP_SerializerSession_trimStrings:
202               return trimStrings(cvt(value, Boolean.class));
203            case PROP_addBeanTypes, PROP_SerializerSession_addBeanTypes:
204               return addBeanTypes(cvt(value, Boolean.class));
205            case PROP_addRootType, PROP_SerializerSession_addRootType:
206               return addRootType(cvt(value, Boolean.class));
207            case PROP_sortCollections, PROP_SerializerSession_sortCollections:
208               return sortCollections(cvt(value, Boolean.class));
209            case PROP_sortMaps, PROP_SerializerSession_sortMaps:
210               return sortMaps(cvt(value, Boolean.class));
211            case PROP_trimEmptyCollections, PROP_SerializerSession_trimEmptyCollections:
212               return trimEmptyCollections(cvt(value, Boolean.class));
213            case PROP_trimEmptyMaps, PROP_SerializerSession_trimEmptyMaps:
214               return trimEmptyMaps(cvt(value, Boolean.class));
215            default:
216               super.property(key, value);
217               return self();
218         }
219      }
220
221      /**
222       * String variable resolver.
223       *
224       * <p>
225       * If not specified, defaults to session created by {@link VarResolver#DEFAULT}.
226       *
227       * @param value
228       *    The new property value.
229       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (value will not be set, defaults to session created by {@link VarResolver#DEFAULT} when accessed).
230       * @return This object.
231       */
232      public SELF resolver(VarResolverSession value) {
233         if (nn(value))
234            resolver = value;
235         return self();
236      }
237
238      /**
239       * HTTP-part schema.
240       *
241       * <p>
242       * Used for schema-based serializers and parsers to define additional formatting.
243       *
244       * @param value
245       *    The new value for this property.
246       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (value will not be set, existing value will be kept).
247       * @return This object.
248       */
249      public SELF schema(HttpPartSchema value) {
250         if (nn(value))
251            schema = value;
252         return self();
253      }
254
255      /**
256       * Same as {@link #schema(HttpPartSchema)} but doesn't overwrite the value if it is already set.
257       *
258       * @param value
259       *    The new value for this property.
260       *    <br>If <jk>null</jk>, then the locale defined on the context is used.
261       * @return This object.
262       */
263      public SELF schemaDefault(HttpPartSchema value) {
264         if (nn(value) && schema == null)
265            schema = value;
266         return self();
267      }
268
269      /**
270       * URI context bean.
271       *
272       * <p>
273       * Bean used for resolution of URIs to absolute or root-relative form.
274       *
275       * <p>
276       * If not specified, defaults to {@link Serializer.Builder#uriContext(UriContext)}.
277       *
278       * @param value
279       *    The new property value.
280       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (value will not be set, defaults to {@link Serializer.Builder#uriContext(UriContext)} from context).
281       * @return This object.
282       */
283      public SELF uriContext(UriContext value) {
284         if (nn(value))
285            uriContext = value;
286         return self();
287      }
288
289      /**
290       * Keep null bean property values.
291       *
292       * @param value The new value for this property.
293       * @return This object.
294       */
295      public SELF keepNullProperties(boolean value) {
296         keepNullProperties = value;
297         return self();
298      }
299
300      /**
301       * Non-default inclusion.
302       *
303       * @param value The new value for this property.
304       * @return This object.
305       * @since 10.0.0
306       */
307      public SELF nonDefault(boolean value) {
308         nonDefault = value;
309         return self();
310      }
311
312      /**
313       * Trim strings.
314       *
315       * @param value The new value for this property.
316       * @return This object.
317       */
318      public SELF trimStrings(boolean value) {
319         trimStrings = value;
320         return self();
321      }
322
323      /**
324       * Add bean types.
325       *
326       * @param value The new value for this property.
327       * @return This object.
328       */
329      public SELF addBeanTypes(boolean value) {
330         addBeanTypes = value;
331         return self();
332      }
333
334      /**
335       * Add root type.
336       *
337       * @param value The new value for this property.
338       * @return This object.
339       */
340      public SELF addRootType(boolean value) {
341         addRootType = value;
342         return self();
343      }
344
345      /**
346       * Sort collections.
347       *
348       * @param value The new value for this property.
349       * @return This object.
350       */
351      public SELF sortCollections(boolean value) {
352         sortCollections = value;
353         return self();
354      }
355
356      /**
357       * Sort maps.
358       *
359       * @param value The new value for this property.
360       * @return This object.
361       */
362      public SELF sortMaps(boolean value) {
363         sortMaps = value;
364         return self();
365      }
366
367      /**
368       * Trim empty collections.
369       *
370       * @param value The new value for this property.
371       * @return This object.
372       */
373      public SELF trimEmptyCollections(boolean value) {
374         trimEmptyCollections = value;
375         return self();
376      }
377
378      /**
379       * Trim empty maps.
380       *
381       * @param value The new value for this property.
382       * @return This object.
383       */
384      public SELF trimEmptyMaps(boolean value) {
385         trimEmptyMaps = value;
386         return self();
387      }
388   }
389
390   /**
391    * Concrete default builder leaf for the non-subclassed {@code create()} path (CRTP terminal).
392    */
393   public static final class DefaultBuilder extends Builder<DefaultBuilder> {
394
395      DefaultBuilder(Serializer ctx) {
396         super(ctx);
397      }
398   }
399
400   /**
401    * Creates a new builder for this object.
402    *
403    * @param ctx The context creating this session.
404    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
405    * @return A new builder.
406    */
407   @SuppressWarnings({
408      "java:S1452" // Builder<?> wildcard return intentional; callers use it to construct session instances polymorphically
409   })
410   public static Builder<?> create(Serializer ctx) {
411      return new DefaultBuilder(assertArgNotNull("ctx", ctx));
412   }
413
414   /**
415    * Create a "_type" property that contains the dictionary name of the bean.
416    *
417    * @param m The bean map to create a class property on.
418    * @param typeName The type name of the bean.
419    * @return A new bean property value.
420    */
421   protected static final BeanPropertyValue createBeanTypeNameProperty(BeanMap<?> m, String typeName) {
422      BeanMeta<?> bm = m.getMeta();
423      return new BeanPropertyValue(bm.getTypeProperty(), bm.getTypeProperty().getName(), typeName, null);
424   }
425
426   /**
427    * Converts the specified throwable to either a {@link RuntimeException} or {@link SerializeException}.
428    *
429    * @param <T> The throwable type.
430    * @param causedBy The exception to cast or wrap.
431    */
432   protected static <T extends Throwable> void handleThrown(T causedBy) {
433      if (causedBy instanceof StackOverflowError)
434         throw new SerializeException(
435            "Stack overflow occurred.  This can occur when trying to serialize models containing loops.  It's recommended you use the MarshallingTraverseContext.BEANTRAVERSE_detectRecursions setting to help locate the loop.");
436      if (causedBy instanceof Error causedBy2)
437         throw causedBy2;
438      if (causedBy instanceof RuntimeException causedBy2)
439         throw causedBy2;
440      if (causedBy instanceof SerializeException causedBy2)
441         throw causedBy2;
442      throw new SerializeException(causedBy);
443   }
444
445   /**
446    * Converts the contents of the specified object array to a list.
447    *
448    * <p>
449    * Works on both object and primitive arrays.
450    *
451    * <p>
452    * In the case of multi-dimensional arrays, the outgoing list will contain elements of type n-1 dimension.
453    * i.e. if {@code type} is <code><jk>int</jk>[][]</code> then {@code list} will have entries of type
454    * <code><jk>int</jk>[]</code>.
455    *
456    * @param type The type of array.
457    * @param array The array being converted.
458    * @return The array as a list.
459    */
460   protected static final List<Object> toList(Class<?> type, Object array) {
461      var componentType = type.getComponentType();
462      if (componentType.isPrimitive()) {
463         var l = Array.getLength(array);
464         var list = new ArrayList<>(l);
465         for (var i = 0; i < l; i++)
466            list.add(Array.get(array, i));
467         return list;
468      }
469      return l((Object[])array);
470   }
471
472   private final HttpPartSchema schema;
473   private final Method javaMethod;                                                // Java method that invoked this serializer.
474   private final Serializer ctx;
475   private final SerializerListener listener;
476   private final UriResolver uriResolver;
477   private VarResolverSession vrs;
478   private final boolean keepNullProperties;
479   private final boolean nonDefault;
480   private final boolean trimStrings;
481   private final boolean addBeanTypes;
482   private final boolean addRootType;
483   private final boolean sortCollections;
484   private final boolean sortMaps;
485   private final boolean trimEmptyCollections;
486   private final boolean trimEmptyMaps;
487
488   /**
489    * Constructor.
490    *
491    * @param builder The builder for this object.
492    */
493   protected SerializerSession(Builder<?> builder) {
494      super(builder);
495      ctx = builder.ctx;
496      javaMethod = builder.javaMethod;
497      schema = builder.schema;
498      UriContext uriContext = builder.uriContext;
499      uriResolver = UriResolver.of(ctx.getUriResolution(), ctx.getUriRelativity(), uriContext);
500      vrs = builder.resolver;
501      listener = BeanInstantiator.createOrNull(ctx.getListener());
502      keepNullProperties = builder.keepNullProperties;
503      nonDefault = builder.nonDefault;
504      trimStrings = builder.trimStrings;
505      addBeanTypes = builder.addBeanTypes;
506      addRootType = builder.addRootType;
507      sortCollections = builder.sortCollections;
508      sortMaps = builder.sortMaps;
509      trimEmptyCollections = builder.trimEmptyCollections;
510      trimEmptyMaps = builder.trimEmptyMaps;
511   }
512
513   /**
514    * Adds a session object to the {@link VarResolverSession} in this session.
515    *
516    * @param <T> The bean type.
517    * @param c The bean type being added.
518    * @param value The bean being added.
519    * @return This object.
520    */
521   public <T> SerializerSession addVarBean(Class<T> c, T value) {
522      getVarResolver().bean(c, value);
523      return this;
524   }
525
526   /**
527    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the specified value should not be serialized.
528    *
529    * @param cm The class type of the object being serialized.
530    * @param attrName The bean attribute name, or <jk>null</jk> if this isn't a bean attribute.
531    * @param value The object being serialized.
532    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the specified value should not be serialized.
533    * @throws SerializeException If recursion occurred.
534    */
535   @SuppressWarnings({
536      "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for value ignore logic with multiple conditions
537   })
538   public final boolean canIgnoreValue(ClassMeta<?> cm, String attrName, Object value) throws SerializeException {
539
540      if (value == null && ! isKeepNullProperties())
541         return true;
542
543      if (value == null)
544         return false;
545
546      if (cm == null)
547         cm = object();
548
549      if (isTrimEmptyCollections()) {
550         if ((cm.isArray() || (cm.isObject() && isArray(value))) && ((Object[])value).length == 0)
551            return true;
552         if ((cm.isCollection() || (cm.isObject() && info(value).isAssignableTo(Collection.class))) && ((Collection<?>)value).isEmpty())
553            return true;
554      }
555
556      if (isTrimEmptyMaps() && (cm.isMap() || (cm.isObject() && info(value).isAssignableTo(Map.class))) && ((Map<?,?>)value).isEmpty())
557         return true;
558
559      try {
560         if ((! isKeepNullProperties()) && (willRecurse(attrName, value, cm) || willExceedDepth()))
561            return true;
562      } catch (MarshallingRecursionException e) {
563         throw new SerializeException(e);
564      }
565
566      return false;
567   }
568
569   /**
570    * Per-bean-property variant of {@link #canIgnoreValue(ClassMeta, String, Object)}.
571    *
572    * <p>
573    * Applies the same standard inclusion rules ({@link Serializer.Builder#keepNullProperties() keepNullProperties},
574    * {@link Serializer.Builder#trimEmptyCollections() trimEmptyCollections} /
575    * {@link Serializer.Builder#trimEmptyMaps() trimEmptyMaps},
576    * recursion detection), then additionally:
577    * <ul>
578    *    <li>Treats {@link Optional#empty()} (and its primitive cousins
579    *       {@link OptionalInt}/{@link OptionalLong}/{@link OptionalDouble}) as a
580    *       <jk>null</jk> property value for inclusion purposes — the shared Optional/absent contract.
581    *    <li>Applies {@link Serializer.Builder#nonDefault() nonDefault} by comparing the surviving value against a
582    *       cached <i>reference instance</i> of the property's containing bean.  When no reference instance can be
583    *       built (no accessible no-arg ctor / instantiation throws), {@code nonDefault} is silently skipped for
584    *       that bean — the property is emitted normally rather than failing.
585    * </ul>
586    *
587    * <p>
588    * Precedence (first "omit" wins): (1) <jk>null</jk> / empty Optional, (2) trimEmpty*, (3) value-equals-default.
589    *
590    * @param pMeta The bean property meta for the value being considered.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
591    * @param attrName The bean attribute name.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
592    * @param value The current property value.
593    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the property should be omitted from the output.
594    * @throws SerializeException If recursion occurred.
595    * @since 10.0.0
596    */
597   public final boolean canIgnoreValue(BeanPropertyMeta pMeta, String attrName, Object value) throws SerializeException {
598      var cMeta = (ClassMeta<?>) pMeta.getBeanInfo();
599      // Resolve Optional<empty> / OptionalInt/Long/Double.isPresent()==false to null for the inclusion check.
600      var resolved = unwrapEmptyOptional(value);
601      if (canIgnoreValue(cMeta, attrName, resolved))
602         return true;
603      if (isNonDefault() && resolved != null)
604         return equalsBeanPropertyDefault(pMeta, attrName, resolved);
605      return false;
606   }
607
608   /**
609    * Resolves an {@link Optional#empty()} (or the empty form of an {@link OptionalInt}/{@link OptionalLong}/
610    * {@link OptionalDouble}) to <jk>null</jk>, leaving all other values unchanged.
611    *
612    * <p>
613    * Nested {@code Optional<Optional<X>>} values are unwrapped recursively.
614    *
615    * @param value The value to resolve.
616    * @return The resolved value (may be <jk>null</jk>).
617    */
618   private static Object unwrapEmptyOptional(Object value) {
619      if (value == null)
620         return null;
621      if (value instanceof Optional<?> o)
622         return o.isEmpty() ? null : unwrapEmptyOptional(o.orElse(null));
623      if (value instanceof OptionalInt oi)
624         return oi.isEmpty() ? null : Integer.valueOf(oi.getAsInt());
625      if (value instanceof OptionalLong ol)
626         return ol.isEmpty() ? null : Long.valueOf(ol.getAsLong());
627      if (value instanceof OptionalDouble od)
628         return od.isEmpty() ? null : Double.valueOf(od.getAsDouble());
629      return value;
630   }
631
632   /**
633    * Returns <jk>true</jk> when {@code value} equals the property's bean-constructed default per the
634    * {@code nonDefault} contract.
635    *
636    * <p>
637    * Resolves (and caches per-{@link ClassMeta}) the containing bean's reference instance.  Returns
638    * <jk>false</jk> when no reference instance can be built (which signals "skip nonDefault for this bean" per
639    * the documented contract).
640    *
641    * @param pMeta The bean-property meta.
642    * @param attrName The bean attribute name.
643    * @param value The surviving property value (non-null).
644    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the value equals the property's default.
645    */
646   private boolean equalsBeanPropertyDefault(BeanPropertyMeta pMeta, String attrName, Object value) {
647      var owning = pMeta.getBeanMeta();
648      if (owning == null)
649         return false;
650      var ownerClass = owning.getClassInfo();
651      if (ownerClass == null)
652         return false;
653      var ownerMeta = getClassMeta(ownerClass.inner());
654      var refValueHolder = ownerMeta.getProperty(NON_DEFAULT_PROPERTY_CACHE_KEY, cm -> {
655         var instance = BeanInstantiator.createOrNull(cm.inner());
656         return instance == null ? NO_REFERENCE_INSTANCE : instance;
657      });
658      var ref = refValueHolder.orElse(null);
659      if (ref == null || ref == NO_REFERENCE_INSTANCE)
660         return false;
661      // Resolve the default value via the bean's accessor surface so it composes with @MarshalledProp
662      // transforms.  Wrap any throw in a guard — a bean whose getter throws on a fresh instance is just
663      // treated as "no default available", which is safer than aborting the serialize.
664      Object defaultValue;
665      try {
666         defaultValue = pMeta.get(toBeanMap(ref), attrName);
667      } catch (RuntimeException e) {  // HTT — defensive: getter on reference instance throws.
668         return false;
669      }
670      var resolvedDefault = unwrapEmptyOptional(defaultValue);
671      return defaultEquals(value, resolvedDefault);
672   }
673
674   /**
675    * {@code Objects.equals} with a numeric-value shortcut so {@code 1.0} equals {@code 1},
676    * {@code BigDecimal("1.0")} equals {@code BigDecimal("1")}, and {@code 0.0}/{@code -0.0} equal each other.
677    *
678    * @param a First value.
679    * @param b Second value.
680    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the values are equal under the {@code nonDefault} contract.
681    */
682   @SuppressWarnings({
683      "java:S1244", // Float equality is intentional: caller has already narrowed to numeric short-circuit; BigDecimal compareTo handles the value-equality semantics.
684      "java:S3776"  // Cognitive complexity acceptable for the numeric/NaN value-equality logic.
685   })
686   private static boolean defaultEquals(Object a, Object b) {
687      if (a == b)
688         return true;
689      if (a == null || b == null)
690         return false;
691      if (a instanceof Number an && b instanceof Number bn) {
692         // Treat NaN as equal to NaN (Objects.equals semantics) so two NaN-typed defaults match.
693         var anan = (an instanceof Double d && Double.isNaN(d)) || (an instanceof Float f && Float.isNaN(f));
694         var bnan = (bn instanceof Double d && Double.isNaN(d)) || (bn instanceof Float f && Float.isNaN(f));
695         if (anan || bnan)
696            return anan && bnan;
697         try {
698            return toBigDecimal(an).compareTo(toBigDecimal(bn)) == 0;
699         } catch (NumberFormatException e) {  // HTT — defensive: a Number subclass whose toString() isn't a valid decimal.
700            return Objects.equals(a, b);
701         }
702      }
703      return Objects.equals(a, b);
704   }
705
706   private static BigDecimal toBigDecimal(Number n) {
707      if (n instanceof BigDecimal bd)
708         return bd;
709      if (n instanceof BigInteger bi)
710         return new BigDecimal(bi);
711      if (n instanceof Float || n instanceof Double)
712         return BigDecimal.valueOf(n.doubleValue());
713      return BigDecimal.valueOf(n.longValue());
714   }
715
716   /** Cache key under which the per-{@link ClassMeta} reference instance is memoized for {@code nonDefault}. */
717   private static final String NON_DEFAULT_PROPERTY_CACHE_KEY = "SerializerSession.nonDefault.referenceInstance";
718   /** Sentinel stored when a reference instance could not be built (cached so we don't retry on every call). */
719   private static final Object NO_REFERENCE_INSTANCE = new Object();
720
721   /**
722    * Consumes each entry in the list.
723    *
724    * @param <E> The element type.
725    * @param c The collection being sorted.
726    * @param consumer The entry consumer.
727    */
728   public final <E> void forEachEntry(Collection<E> c, Consumer<E> consumer) {
729      if (isEmpty(c))
730         return;
731      if (isSortCollections() && !(c instanceof SortedSet) && isSortable(c))
732         c.stream().sorted().forEach(consumer);
733      else
734         c.forEach(consumer);
735   }
736
737   /**
738    * Consumes each map entry in the map.
739    *
740    * @param <K> The key type.
741    * @param <V> The value type.
742    * @param m The map being consumed.
743    * @param consumer The map entry consumer.
744    */
745   @SuppressWarnings({
746      "cast" // Cast required for generic type safety
747   })
748   public final <K,V> void forEachEntry(Map<K,V> m, Consumer<Map.Entry<K,V>> consumer) {
749      if (isEmpty(m))
750         return;
751      if (isSortMaps() && !(m instanceof SortedMap) && isSortable(m.keySet()))
752         ((Map)m).entrySet().stream().sorted(Map.Entry.comparingByKey()).forEach(x -> consumer.accept((Map.Entry<K,V>)x));
753      else
754         m.entrySet().forEach(consumer);
755   }
756
757   /**
758    * Iterates over a streamable object (Iterator, Iterable, or Stream), consuming each entry.
759    *
760    * <p>
761    * For collections, delegates to {@link #forEachEntry(Collection, Consumer)} which supports sorting.
762    * For other streamable types, iterates lazily without materializing into a List.
763    *
764    * @param o The streamable object.
765    * @param type The class meta for the object.
766    * @param consumer The entry consumer.
767    * @since 9.2.1
768    */
769   @SuppressWarnings({
770      "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for streamable-entry dispatch logic
771   })
772   public final void forEachStreamableEntry(Object o, ClassMeta<?> type, Consumer consumer) {
773      if (o == null)
774         return;
775      if (type.isCollection()) {
776         forEachEntry((Collection)o, consumer);
777      } else if (type.isIterable()) {
778         if (o instanceof BeanSupplier bs) {
779            try {
780               drainBeanSupplier(bs, consumer);
781            } catch (RuntimeException e) {
782               throw e;
783            } catch (Exception e) {
784               throw new BasicRuntimeException(e);
785            }
786         } else {
787            ((Iterable)o).forEach(consumer);
788         }
789      } else if (type.isIterator()) {
790         if (o instanceof Enumeration e)
791            e.asIterator().forEachRemaining(consumer);
792         else
793            ((Iterator)o).forEachRemaining(consumer);
794      } else if (type.isStream()) {
795         try (var stream = (Stream)o) {
796            stream.forEach(consumer);
797         }
798      }
799   }
800
801   private static void drainBeanSupplier(BeanSupplier bs, Consumer consumer) throws Exception {
802      bs.begin();
803      try {
804         bs.iterator().forEachRemaining(consumer);
805      } catch (Exception e) {
806         bs.onError(e);
807      } finally {
808         bs.complete();
809      }
810   }
811
812   /**
813    * Converts a streamable object (Iterator, Iterable, or Stream) to a List.
814    *
815    * <p>
816    * Used by serializers that need to know the collection size or inspect elements before serializing
817    * (e.g. MsgPack, HTML, CSV).
818    *
819    * @param o The streamable object.
820    * @param type The class meta for the object.
821    * @return A new list containing all elements.
822    * @since 9.2.1
823    */
824   public final List<Object> toListFromStreamable(Object o, ClassMeta<?> type) {
825      var list = new ArrayList<>();
826      forEachStreamableEntry(o, type, list::add);
827      return list;
828   }
829
830   /**
831    * Returns the listener associated with this session.
832    *
833    * @return The listener associated with this session, or <jk>null</jk> if there is no listener.
834    */
835   public SerializerListener getListener() { return listener; }
836
837   /**
838    * Returns the listener associated with this session.
839    *
840    * @param <T> The listener type.
841    * @param c The listener class to cast to.
842    * @return The listener associated with this session, or <jk>null</jk> if there is no listener.
843    */
844   public <T extends SerializerListener> T getListener(Class<T> c) {
845      return (T)listener;
846   }
847
848   /**
849    * Optional method that specifies HTTP request headers for this serializer.
850    *
851    * <p>
852    * For example, {@link SoapXmlSerializer} needs to set a <c>SOAPAction</c> header.
853    *
854    * <p>
855    * This method is typically meaningless if the serializer is being used stand-alone (i.e. outside of a REST server
856    * or client).
857    *
858    * <p>
859    * The default implementation of this method simply calls {@link Serializer#getResponseHeaders(SerializerSession)}.
860    *
861    * @return
862    *    The HTTP headers to set on HTTP requests.
863    *    Never <jk>null</jk>.
864    */
865   public Map<String,String> getResponseHeaders() { return ctx.getResponseHeaders(this); }
866
867   /**
868    * HTTP part schema of object being serialized.
869    *
870    * @return HTTP part schema of object being serialized, or <jk>null</jk> if not specified.
871    */
872   public final HttpPartSchema getSchema() { return schema; }
873
874   /**
875    * Returns the variable resolver session.
876    *
877    * @return The variable resolver session.
878    */
879   public VarResolverSession getVarResolver() {
880      if (vrs == null)
881         vrs = createDefaultVarResolverSession();
882      return vrs;
883   }
884
885   /**
886    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this serializer subclasses from {@link WriterSerializer}.
887    *
888    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this serializer subclasses from {@link WriterSerializer}.
889    */
890   public boolean isWriterSerializer() { return false; }
891
892   /**
893    * Resolves any variables in the specified string.
894    *
895    * @param string The string to resolve values in.
896    * @return The string with variables resolved.
897    */
898   public String resolve(String string) {
899      return getVarResolver().resolve(string);
900   }
901
902   /**
903    * Converts a String to an absolute URI based on the {@link UriContext} on this session.
904    *
905    * @param uri
906    *    The input URI.
907    *    Can be any of the following:
908    *    <ul>
909    *       <li>{@link URI}
910    *       <li>{@link URL}
911    *       <li>{@link CharSequence}
912    *    </ul>
913    *    URI can be any of the following forms:
914    *    <ul>
915    *       <li><js>"foo://foo"</js> - Absolute URI.
916    *       <li><js>"/foo"</js> - Root-relative URI.
917    *       <li><js>"/"</js> - Root URI.
918    *       <li><js>"context:/foo"</js> - Context-root-relative URI.
919    *       <li><js>"context:/"</js> - Context-root URI.
920    *       <li><js>"servlet:/foo"</js> - Servlet-path-relative URI.
921    *       <li><js>"servlet:/"</js> - Servlet-path URI.
922    *       <li><js>"request:/foo"</js> - Request-path-relative URI.
923    *       <li><js>"request:/"</js> - Request-path URI.
924    *       <li><js>"foo"</js> - Path-info-relative URI.
925    *       <li><js>""</js> - Path-info URI.
926    *    </ul>
927    * @return The resolved URI.
928    */
929   public final String resolveUri(Object uri) {
930      return uriResolver.resolve(uri);
931   }
932
933   /**
934    * Shortcut method for serializing objects directly to either a <c>String</c> or <code><jk>byte</jk>[]</code>
935    * depending on the serializer type.
936    *
937    * @param o The object to serialize.
938    * @return
939    *    The serialized object.
940    *    <br>Character-based serializers will return a <c>String</c>.
941    *    <br>Stream-based serializers will return a <code><jk>byte</jk>[]</code>.
942    * @throws SerializeException If a problem occurred trying to convert the output.
943    */
944   public Object serialize(Object o) throws SerializeException {
945      throw uoex();
946   }
947
948   /**
949    * Serialize the specified object using the specified session.
950    *
951    * @param out Where to send the output from the serializer.
952    * @param o The object to serialize.
953    * @throws SerializeException If a problem occurred trying to convert the output.
954    * @throws IOException Thrown by the underlying stream.
955    */
956   public final void serialize(Object o, Object out) throws SerializeException, IOException {
957      try (SerializerPipe pipe = createPipe(out)) {
958         var unwrapped = unwrapSupplier(o, 0);
959         if (unwrapped instanceof BeanConsumer && !(unwrapped instanceof BeanChannel))
960            throw new SerializeException(this,
961               "BeanConsumer cannot be used as a serializer source. Use BeanSupplier or BeanChannel for round-trip support.");
962         doSerialize(pipe, unwrapped);
963      } catch (SerializeException | IOException e) {
964         throw e;
965      } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") StackOverflowError e) {
966         throw new SerializeException(this,
967            "Stack overflow occurred.  This can occur when trying to serialize models containing loops.  It's recommended you use the MarshallingTraverseContext.BEANTRAVERSE_detectRecursions setting to help locate the loop.");
968      } catch (Exception e) {
969         throw new SerializeException(this, e);
970      } finally {
971         checkForWarnings();
972      }
973   }
974
975   /**
976    * Recursively unwraps nested {@link Supplier} chains to their underlying value.
977    *
978    * <p>
979    * Supports chains of the form {@code Supplier<Supplier<T>>} → {@code T} with a maximum depth of 10
980    * to prevent infinite loops. Note that {@link BeanSupplier} instances are NOT unwrapped here —
981    * they are treated as {@link Iterable} sequences by the serializer.
982    *
983    * @param o The object to unwrap.
984    * @param depth The current recursion depth (must be 0 on initial call).
985    * @return The unwrapped value, or the original object if it is not a {@code Supplier}.
986    * @throws SerializeException If the {@link Supplier} chain exceeds 10 levels.
987    */
988   protected final Object unwrapSupplier(Object o, int depth) throws SerializeException {
989      if (! (o instanceof Supplier<?>) || o instanceof BeanSupplier)
990         return o;
991      if (depth > 10)
992         throw new SerializeException(this, "Supplier chain exceeds maximum unwrap depth of 10.");
993      return unwrapSupplier(((Supplier<?>)o).get(), depth + 1);
994   }
995
996   /**
997    * Shortcut method for serializing an object to a String.
998    *
999    * @param o The object to serialize.
1000    * @return
1001    *    The serialized object.
1002    *    <br>Character-based serializers will return a <c>String</c>
1003    *    <br>Stream-based serializers will return a <code><jk>byte</jk>[]</code> converted to a string based on the {@link OutputStreamSerializer.Builder#binaryFormat(BinaryFormat)} setting.
1004    * @throws SerializeException If a problem occurred trying to convert the output.
1005    */
1006   public String serializeToString(Object o) throws SerializeException {
1007      throw uoex();
1008   }
1009
1010   /**
1011    * Sorts the specified collection if {@link SerializerSession#isSortCollections()} returns <jk>true</jk>.
1012    *
1013    * @param <E> The element type.
1014    * @param c The collection being sorted.
1015    * @return A new sorted {@link TreeSet}.
1016    */
1017   public final <E> Collection<E> sort(Collection<E> c) {
1018      if (isEmpty(c) || c instanceof SortedSet)
1019         return c;
1020      if (isSortCollections() && isSortable(c))
1021         return c.stream().sorted().toList();
1022      return c;
1023   }
1024
1025   /**
1026    * Sorts the specified collection if {@link SerializerSession#isSortCollections()} returns <jk>true</jk>.
1027    *
1028    * @param <E> The element type.
1029    * @param c The collection being sorted.
1030    * @return A new sorted {@link TreeSet}.
1031    */
1032   public final <E> List<E> sort(List<E> c) {
1033      if (isEmpty(c))
1034         return c;
1035      if (isSortCollections() && isSortable(c))
1036         return c.stream().sorted().toList();
1037      return c;
1038   }
1039
1040   /**
1041    * Sorts the specified map if {@link SerializerSession#isSortMaps()} returns <jk>true</jk>.
1042    *
1043    * @param <K> The key type.
1044    * @param <V> The value type.
1045    * @param m The map being sorted.
1046    * @return A new sorted {@link TreeMap}.
1047    */
1048   public final <K,V> Map<K,V> sort(Map<K,V> m) {
1049      if (isEmpty(m) || m instanceof SortedMap)
1050         return m;
1051      if (isSortMaps() && isSortable(m.keySet()))
1052         return new TreeMap<>(m);
1053      return m;
1054   }
1055
1056   /**
1057    * Converts the specified object to a <c>String</c>.
1058    *
1059    * <p>
1060    * Also has the following effects:
1061    * <ul>
1062    *    <li><c>Class</c> object is converted to a readable name.  See {@link ClassInfo#getNameFull()}.
1063    *    <li>Whitespace is trimmed if the trim-strings setting is enabled.
1064    * </ul>
1065    *
1066    * @param o The object to convert to a <c>String</c>.
1067    * @return The object converted to a String, or <jk>null</jk> if the input was <jk>null</jk>.
1068    */
1069   public final String toString(Object o) {
1070      if (o == null)
1071         return null;
1072      if (o.getClass() == Class.class)
1073         return info((Class<?>)o).getNameFull();
1074      if (o.getClass() == ClassInfo.class)
1075         return ((ClassInfo)o).getNameFull();
1076      if (o.getClass().isEnum())
1077         return getClassMetaForObject(o).toString(o);
1078      var cm = getClassMetaForObject(o);
1079      if (cm.isDate())
1080         return serializeDate((Date)o, cm);
1081      if (cm.isCalendar())
1082         return serializeCalendar(o, cm);
1083      if (cm.isTemporal())
1084         return serializeTemporal((TemporalAccessor)o, cm);
1085      if (cm.isDuration())
1086         return serializeDuration((Duration)o);
1087      if (cm.isPeriod())
1088         return serializePeriod((Period)o);
1089      var s = o.toString();
1090      if (isTrimStrings())
1091         s = s.trim();
1092      return s;
1093   }
1094
1095   /**
1096    * Formats a {@link Duration} value using this session's configured
1097    * {@linkplain #getDurationFormat() duration format}.
1098    *
1099    * @param d The value to format. <jk>null</jk> returns <jk>null</jk>.
1100    * @return The formatted value, or <jk>null</jk> if {@code d} is <jk>null</jk>.
1101    */
1102   protected final String serializeDuration(Duration d) {
1103      return Iso8601Utils.formatDuration(d, getDurationFormat());
1104   }
1105
1106   /**
1107    * Formats a {@link Period} value using this session's configured
1108    * {@linkplain #getPeriodFormat() period format}.
1109    *
1110    * @param p The value to format. <jk>null</jk> returns <jk>null</jk>.
1111    * @return The formatted value, or <jk>null</jk> if {@code p} is <jk>null</jk>.
1112    */
1113   protected final String serializePeriod(Period p) {
1114      return Iso8601Utils.formatPeriod(p, getPeriodFormat());
1115   }
1116
1117   /**
1118    * Formats a {@link Date} value using this session's configured
1119    * {@linkplain #getDateFormat() date format} and {@linkplain #getTimeZone() time zone}.
1120    *
1121    * @param d The value to format. <jk>null</jk> returns <jk>null</jk>.
1122    * @param cm The class metadata for the value (kept for symmetry with {@link Iso8601Utils#formatDate}).
1123    * @return The formatted value, or <jk>null</jk> if {@code d} is <jk>null</jk>.
1124    */
1125   protected final String serializeDate(Date d, ClassMeta<?> cm) {
1126      return Iso8601Utils.formatDate(d, cm, getDateFormat(), getTimeZone());
1127   }
1128
1129   /**
1130    * Formats a {@link Calendar} or {@link XMLGregorianCalendar} value using this
1131    * session's configured {@linkplain #getCalendarFormat() calendar format} and
1132    * {@linkplain #getTimeZone() time zone}.
1133    *
1134    * <p>
1135    * {@link XMLGregorianCalendar} values always emit their
1136    * {@link XMLGregorianCalendar#toXMLFormat()} form regardless of the configured
1137    * format, mirroring the parse-side rule.
1138    *
1139    * @param c The value to format. Must be a {@link Calendar} or {@link XMLGregorianCalendar}.
1140    *    <jk>null</jk> returns <jk>null</jk>.
1141    * @param cm The class metadata for the value (kept for symmetry with {@link Iso8601Utils#formatCalendar}).
1142    * @return The formatted value, or <jk>null</jk> if {@code c} is <jk>null</jk>.
1143    */
1144   protected final String serializeCalendar(Object c, ClassMeta<?> cm) {
1145      return Iso8601Utils.formatCalendar(c, cm, getCalendarFormat(), getTimeZone());
1146   }
1147
1148   /**
1149    * Formats a {@link TemporalAccessor} value using this session's configured
1150    * {@linkplain #getTemporalFormat() temporal format} and {@linkplain #getTimeZone() time zone}.
1151    *
1152    * @param t The value to format. Must be a {@link Temporal} subtype.
1153    *    <jk>null</jk> returns <jk>null</jk>.
1154    * @param cm The class metadata for the value (kept for symmetry with {@link Iso8601Utils#formatTemporal}).
1155    * @return The formatted value, or <jk>null</jk> if {@code t} is <jk>null</jk>.
1156    */
1157   protected final String serializeTemporal(TemporalAccessor t, ClassMeta<?> cm) {
1158      return Iso8601Utils.formatTemporal(t, cm, getTemporalFormat(), getTimeZone());
1159   }
1160
1161   /**
1162    * Trims the specified string if {@link SerializerSession#isTrimStrings()} returns <jk>true</jk>.
1163    *
1164    * @param o The input string to trim.
1165    * @return The trimmed string, or <jk>null</jk> if the input was <jk>null</jk>.
1166    */
1167   public final String trim(Object o) {
1168      if (o == null)
1169         return null;
1170      var s = o.toString();
1171      if (isTrimStrings())
1172         s = s.trim();
1173      return s;
1174   }
1175
1176   private static boolean isSortable(Collection<?> c) {
1177      if (c == null)
1178         return false;
1179      for (var o : c)
1180         if (! (o instanceof Comparable))
1181            return false;
1182      return true;
1183   }
1184
1185   /**
1186    * Adds a session object to the {@link VarResolverSession} in this session.
1187    *
1188    * @return This object.
1189    */
1190   protected VarResolverSession createDefaultVarResolverSession() {
1191      return VarResolver.DEFAULT.createSession();
1192   }
1193
1194   /**
1195    * Wraps the specified input object into a {@link ParserPipe} object so that it can be easily converted into
1196    * a stream or reader.
1197    *
1198    * @param output
1199    *    The output location.
1200    *    <br>For character-based serializers, this can be any of the following types:
1201    *    <ul>
1202    *       <li>{@link Writer}
1203    *       <li>{@link OutputStream} - Output will be written as UTF-8 encoded stream.
1204    *       <li>{@link File} - Output will be written as system-default encoded stream.
1205    *       <li>{@link StringBuilder}
1206    *    </ul>
1207    *    <br>For byte-based serializers, this can be any of the following types:
1208    *    <ul>
1209    *       <li>{@link OutputStream}
1210    *       <li>{@link File}
1211    *    </ul>
1212    * @return
1213    *    A new {@link ParserPipe} wrapper around the specified input object.
1214    */
1215   protected SerializerPipe createPipe(Object output) {
1216      return new SerializerPipe(output);
1217   }
1218
1219   /**
1220    * Serializes a POJO to the specified pipe.
1221    *
1222    * <p>
1223    * This method should NOT close the context object.
1224    *
1225    * <p>
1226    * The default implementation of this method simply calls {@link Serializer#doSerialize(SerializerSession,SerializerPipe,Object)}.
1227    *
1228    * @param pipe Where to send the output from the serializer.
1229    * @param o The object to serialize.
1230    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
1231    * @throws SerializeException Problem occurred trying to serialize object.
1232    */
1233   protected void doSerialize(SerializerPipe pipe, Object o) throws IOException, SerializeException {
1234      ctx.doSerialize(this, pipe, o);
1235   }
1236
1237   /**
1238    * Generalize the specified object if a POJO swap is associated with it.
1239    *
1240    * @param o The object to generalize.
1241    * @param type The type of object.
1242    * @return The generalized object, or <jk>null</jk> if the object is <jk>null</jk>.
1243    * @throws SerializeException If a problem occurred trying to convert the output.
1244    */
1245   protected final Object generalize(Object o, ClassMeta<?> type) throws SerializeException {
1246      try {
1247         if (o == null)
1248            return null;
1249         ObjectSwap f = (type == null || type.isObject() || type.isString() ? getClassMeta(o.getClass()).getSwap(this) : type.getSwap(this));
1250         if (f == null)
1251            return o;
1252         return f.swap(this, o);
1253      } catch (SerializeException e) {
1254         throw e;
1255      } catch (Exception e) {
1256         throw new SerializeException(e);
1257      }
1258   }
1259
1260   /**
1261    * Resolves the dictionary name for the actual type.
1262    *
1263    * @param session The current serializer session.
1264    * @param eType The expected type of the bean property.
1265    * @param aType The actual type of the bean property.
1266    * @param pMeta The current bean property being serialized.
1267    * @return The bean dictionary name, or <jk>null</jk> if a name could not be found.
1268    */
1269   @SuppressWarnings({
1270      "null",       // Null analysis handled by runtime checks
1271      "java:S3776"  // Cognitive complexity acceptable for type name resolution logic
1272   })
1273   protected final String getBeanTypeName(SerializerSession session, ClassMeta<?> eType, ClassMeta<?> aType, BeanPropertyMeta pMeta) {
1274      if (eType == aType || ! (isAddBeanTypes() || (session.isRoot() && isAddRootType())))
1275         return null;
1276
1277      String eTypeTn = eType.getBeanDictionaryName();
1278
1279      // First see if it's defined on the actual type.
1280      String tn = aType.getBeanDictionaryName();
1281      if (nn(tn) && ! tn.equals(eTypeTn)) {
1282         return tn;
1283      }
1284
1285      // Then see if it's defined on the expected type.
1286      // The expected type might be an interface with mappings for implementation classes.
1287      BeanRegistry br = eType.getBeanRegistry();
1288      if (nn(br)) {
1289         tn = br.getTypeName(aType);
1290         if (nn(tn) && ! tn.equals(eTypeTn))
1291            return tn;
1292      }
1293
1294      // Then look on the bean property.
1295      br = pMeta == null ? null : (BeanRegistry) pMeta.getBeanRegistry();
1296      if (nn(br)) {
1297         tn = br.getTypeName(aType);
1298         if (nn(tn) && ! tn.equals(eTypeTn))
1299            return tn;
1300      }
1301
1302      // Finally look in the session.
1303      br = getBeanRegistry();
1304      if (nn(br)) {
1305         tn = br.getTypeName(aType);
1306         if (nn(tn) && ! tn.equals(eTypeTn))
1307            return tn;
1308      }
1309
1310      return null;
1311   }
1312
1313   /**
1314    * Returns the parser-side expected type for the object.
1315    *
1316    * <p>
1317    * The return value depends on the {@link Serializer.Builder#addRootType()} setting.
1318    * When disabled, the parser already knows the Java POJO type being parsed, so there is
1319    * no reason to add <js>"_type"</js> attributes to the root-level object.
1320    *
1321    * @param o The object to get the expected type on.
1322    * @return The expected type.
1323    */
1324   @SuppressWarnings({
1325      "java:S1452"  // Wildcard required - ClassMeta<?> for root type metadata
1326   })
1327   protected final ClassMeta<?> getExpectedRootType(Object o) {
1328      if (isAddRootType())
1329         return object();
1330      var cm = getClassMetaForObject(o);
1331      if (nn(cm) && cm.isOptional())
1332         return cm.getElementType();
1333      return cm;
1334   }
1335
1336   /**
1337    * Returns the Java method that invoked this serializer.
1338    *
1339    * <p>
1340    * When using the REST API, this is the Java method invoked by the REST call.
1341    * Can be used to access annotations defined on the method or class.
1342    *
1343    * @return The Java method that invoked this serializer.
1344   */
1345   protected final Method getJavaMethod() { return javaMethod; }
1346
1347   /**
1348    * URI context bean.
1349    *
1350    * @see Serializer.Builder#uriContext(UriContext)
1351    * @return
1352    *    Bean used for resolution of URIs to absolute or root-relative form.
1353    */
1354   protected final UriContext getUriContext() { return ctx.getUriContext(); }
1355
1356   /**
1357    * URI relativity.
1358    *
1359    * @see Serializer.Builder#uriRelativity(UriRelativity)
1360    * @return
1361    *    Defines what relative URIs are relative to when serializing any of the following:
1362    */
1363   protected final UriRelativity getUriRelativity() { return ctx.getUriRelativity(); }
1364
1365   /**
1366    * URI resolution.
1367    *
1368    * @see Serializer.Builder#uriResolution(UriResolution)
1369    * @return
1370    *    Defines the resolution level for URIs when serializing URIs.
1371    */
1372   protected final UriResolution getUriResolution() { return ctx.getUriResolution(); }
1373
1374   /**
1375    * Returns the configured default duration wire format.
1376    *
1377    * @return The configured default duration wire format.
1378    */
1379   protected final DurationFormat getDurationFormat() { return ctx.getMarshallingContext().getDurationFormat(); }
1380
1381   /**
1382    * Returns the configured default period wire format.
1383    *
1384    * @return The configured default period wire format.
1385    */
1386   protected final PeriodFormat getPeriodFormat() { return ctx.getMarshallingContext().getPeriodFormat(); }
1387
1388   /**
1389    * Returns the configured default calendar wire format.
1390    *
1391    * @return The configured default calendar wire format.
1392    */
1393   protected final CalendarFormat getCalendarFormat() { return ctx.getMarshallingContext().getCalendarFormat(); }
1394
1395   /**
1396    * Returns the configured default date wire format.
1397    *
1398    * @return The configured default date wire format.
1399    */
1400   protected final DateFormat getDateFormat() { return ctx.getMarshallingContext().getDateFormat(); }
1401
1402   /**
1403    * Returns the configured default temporal wire format.
1404    *
1405    * @return The configured default temporal wire format.
1406    */
1407   protected final TemporalFormat getTemporalFormat() { return ctx.getMarshallingContext().getTemporalFormat(); }
1408
1409   /**
1410    * Returns the configured default time-zone wire format.
1411    *
1412    * @return The configured default time-zone wire format.
1413    */
1414   protected final TimeZoneFormat getTimeZoneFormat() { return ctx.getMarshallingContext().getTimeZoneFormat(); }
1415
1416   /**
1417    * Returns the configured default locale wire format.
1418    *
1419    * @return The configured default locale wire format.
1420    */
1421   protected final LocaleFormat getLocaleFormat() { return ctx.getMarshallingContext().getLocaleFormat(); }
1422
1423   /**
1424    * Returns the URI resolver.
1425    *
1426    * @return The URI resolver.
1427    */
1428   protected final UriResolver getUriResolver() { return uriResolver; }
1429
1430   /**
1431    * Add <js>"_type"</js> properties when needed.
1432    *
1433    * @see Serializer.Builder#addBeanTypes()
1434    * @return
1435    *    <jk>true</jk> if <js>"_type"</js> properties added to beans if their type cannot be inferred
1436    *    through reflection.
1437    */
1438   protected boolean isAddBeanTypes() { return addBeanTypes; }
1439
1440   /**
1441    * Add type attribute to root nodes.
1442    *
1443    * @see Serializer.Builder#addRootType()
1444    * @return
1445    *    <jk>true</jk> if type property should be added to root node.
1446    */
1447   protected final boolean isAddRootType() { return addRootType; }
1448
1449   /**
1450    * Don't trim null bean property values.
1451    *
1452    * @see Serializer.Builder#keepNullProperties()
1453    * @return
1454    *    <jk>true</jk> if null bean values are serialized to the output.
1455    */
1456   protected final boolean isKeepNullProperties() { return keepNullProperties; }
1457
1458   /**
1459    * Don't serialize bean properties whose values equal the default for their type.
1460    *
1461    * @see Serializer.Builder#nonDefault()
1462    * @return
1463    *    <jk>true</jk> if bean properties whose value equals the type / bean-constructed default are omitted.
1464    * @since 10.0.0
1465    */
1466   protected final boolean isNonDefault() { return nonDefault; }
1467
1468   /**
1469    * Sort arrays and collections alphabetically.
1470    *
1471    * @see Serializer.Builder#sortCollections()
1472    * @return
1473    *    <jk>true</jk> if arrays and collections are copied and sorted before serialization.
1474    */
1475   protected final boolean isSortCollections() { return sortCollections; }
1476
1477   /**
1478    * Sort maps alphabetically.
1479    *
1480    * @see Serializer.Builder#sortMaps()
1481    * @return
1482    *    <jk>true</jk> if maps are copied and sorted before serialization.
1483    */
1484   protected final boolean isSortMaps() { return sortMaps; }
1485
1486   /**
1487    * Trim empty lists and arrays.
1488    *
1489    * @see Serializer.Builder#trimEmptyCollections()
1490    * @return
1491    *    <jk>true</jk> if empty lists and arrays are not serialized to the output.
1492    */
1493   protected final boolean isTrimEmptyCollections() { return trimEmptyCollections; }
1494
1495   /**
1496    * Trim empty maps.
1497    *
1498    * @see Serializer.Builder#trimEmptyMaps()
1499    * @return
1500    *    <jk>true</jk> if empty map values are not serialized to the output.
1501    */
1502   protected final boolean isTrimEmptyMaps() { return trimEmptyMaps; }
1503
1504   /**
1505    * Trim strings.
1506    *
1507    * @see Serializer.Builder#trimStrings()
1508    * @return
1509    *    <jk>true</jk> if string values will be trimmed of whitespace using {@link String#trim()} before being serialized.
1510    */
1511   protected boolean isTrimStrings() { return trimStrings; }
1512
1513   /**
1514    * Specialized warning when an exception is thrown while executing a bean getter.
1515    *
1516    * @param p The bean map entry representing the bean property.
1517    * @param t The throwable that the bean getter threw.
1518    * @throws SerializeException Thrown if ignoreInvocationExceptionOnGetters is false.
1519    */
1520   protected final void onBeanGetterException(BeanPropertyMeta p, Throwable t) throws SerializeException {
1521      // If it's a StackOverflowError, let it propagate so it can be caught by serialize() method
1522      // and converted to a proper SerializeException with recursion detection message
1523      if (t instanceof StackOverflowError t2)
1524         throw t2;
1525
1526      if (nn(listener))
1527         listener.onBeanGetterException(this, t, p);
1528      String prefix = (isDebug() ? getStack(false) + ": " : "");
1529      addWarning("{0}Could not call getValue() on property ''{1}'' of class ''{2}'', exception = {3}", prefix, p.getName(), p.getBeanMeta().getBeanInfo(), localizedMessage(t));
1530      if (! isIgnoreInvocationExceptionsOnGetters())
1531         throw new SerializeException(this, "{0}Could not call getValue() on property ''{1}'' of class ''{2}'', exception = {3}", prefix, p.getName(), p.getBeanMeta().getBeanInfo(), localizedMessage(t))
1532            .initCause(t);
1533   }
1534
1535   /**
1536    * Logs a warning message.
1537    *
1538    * @param t The throwable that was thrown (if there was one).
1539    * @param msg The warning message.
1540    * @param args Optional {@link MessageFormat}-style arguments.
1541    */
1542   @Override
1543   protected void onError(Throwable t, String msg, Object...args) {
1544      if (nn(listener))
1545         listener.onError(this, t, f(msg, args));
1546      super.onError(t, msg, args);
1547   }
1548
1549   @Override /* Overridden from MarshallingTraverseSession */
1550   protected FluentMap<String,Object> properties() {
1551      return super.properties()
1552         .a(PROP_uriResolver, uriResolver);
1553   }
1554
1555   /**
1556    * Same as {@link #push(String, Object, ClassMeta)} but wraps {@link MarshallingRecursionException} inside {@link SerializeException}.
1557    *
1558    * @param attrName The attribute name.
1559    * @param o The current object being traversed.
1560    * @param eType The expected class type.
1561    * @return
1562    *    The {@link ClassMeta} of the object so that <c>instanceof</c> operations only need to be performed
1563    *    once (since they can be expensive).
1564    * @throws SerializeException If recursion occurred.
1565    */
1566   @SuppressWarnings({
1567      "java:S1452"  // Wildcard required - ClassMeta<?> for push stack metadata
1568   })
1569   protected final ClassMeta<?> push2(String attrName, Object o, ClassMeta<?> eType) throws SerializeException {
1570      try {
1571         return super.push(attrName, o, eType);
1572      } catch (MarshallingRecursionException e) {
1573         throw new SerializeException(e);
1574      }
1575   }
1576
1577   /**
1578    * Opposite of {@link #resolveUri(Object)}.
1579    *
1580    * <p>
1581    * Converts the URI to a value relative to the specified <c>relativeTo</c> parameter.
1582    *
1583    * <p>
1584    * Both parameters can be any of the following:
1585    * <ul>
1586    *    <li>{@link URI}
1587    *    <li>{@link URL}
1588    *    <li>{@link CharSequence}
1589    * </ul>
1590    *
1591    * <p>
1592    * Both URIs can be any of the following forms:
1593    * <ul>
1594    *    <li><js>"foo://foo"</js> - Absolute URI.
1595    *    <li><js>"/foo"</js> - Root-relative URI.
1596    *    <li><js>"/"</js> - Root URI.
1597    *    <li><js>"context:/foo"</js> - Context-root-relative URI.
1598    *    <li><js>"context:/"</js> - Context-root URI.
1599    *    <li><js>"servlet:/foo"</js> - Servlet-path-relative URI.
1600    *    <li><js>"servlet:/"</js> - Servlet-path URI.
1601    *    <li><js>"request:/foo"</js> - Request-path-relative URI.
1602    *    <li><js>"request:/"</js> - Request-path URI.
1603    *    <li><js>"foo"</js> - Path-info-relative URI.
1604    *    <li><js>""</js> - Path-info URI.
1605    * </ul>
1606    *
1607    * @param relativeTo The URI to relativize against.
1608    * @param uri The URI to relativize.
1609    * @return The relativized URI.
1610    */
1611   protected final String relativizeUri(Object relativeTo, Object uri) {
1612      return uriResolver.relativize(relativeTo, uri);
1613   }
1614
1615   /**
1616    * Invokes the specified swap on the specified object if the swap is not null.
1617    *
1618    * @param swap The swap to invoke.  Can be <jk>null</jk>.
1619    * @param o The input object.
1620    * @return The swapped object.
1621    * @throws SerializeException If swap method threw an exception.
1622    */
1623   protected Object swap(ObjectSwap swap, Object o) throws SerializeException {
1624      try {
1625         if (swap == null)
1626            return o;
1627         return swap.swap(this, o);
1628      } catch (Exception e) {
1629         throw new SerializeException(e);
1630      }
1631   }
1632}
